title: gouldia elongata var. kahili
intro: Gouldia elongata var. kahili, also known as Kahili Ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of gingerols and shogaols. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as nausea, digestive problems, and menstrual cramps, due to its analgesic and antiemetic properties. In herbal preparations, Kahili Ginger is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
